Wake up.
Look around my quarters. What's here?
Try to sit up in bed.
I hear a faint low humming through the wall. Where's it coming from?
Roll out of bed and check my shelf for personal items.
Take the welcome packet and the mug from the shelf.
Head to the kitchen.
Who's here this early?
Introduce myself. I'm the new colonist.
Describe this person. Every minifigure detail I can see.
Try to pour myself some coffee with these claw hands.
Ask about the colony. Has anyone gone missing recently?
Ask them if they can show me around today.
Follow them through the corridor toward operations.
Look up at the connector tube as we pass. Anything unusual?
Point out the crack to my guide. What do they say?
Enter the operations dome. Meet the operations chief.
Ask the chief about my assignments. What do they give me?
Head to the greenhouse with my guide.
Meet the gardener. Describe them and what they're growing.
Walk in silence down a row of tomato plants.
Head back through the kitchen. An unclaimed package is sitting on a shelf with no label.
Ask whoever's in the kitchen who the package belongs to.
Head into the recreational dome.
There's a pool. Jump in for a swim.
The lifeguard fishes me out. Meet them.
My mug is ruined from the water. The lifeguard hands me a towel.
Who else is in the rec dome?
Ask about any colony gossip or drama.
Head toward the garage. Through a corridor window, a pulsing green light on the horizon catches my eye.
Ask my guide what the pulsing green light is.
Enter the garage. Meet the mechanic.
The mechanic is fumbling with a tiny bolt. Offer to help.
Help them with the rover they're working on.
Try to kneel down and look under the rover.
Pop my head off to peek under the rover from a lower angle.
Feel around. Something's wedged tight. Try to delicately pinch it free with a fingertip.
Use the pliers from my toolkit instead. Pull out a strange small gear piece and keep it.
Show the mechanic the gear. Do they recognize it?
Ask if the rover could fit three of us — me, the mechanic, and my guide.
Head to the canteen for lunch. Full inventory check on the way.
Is the gardener at lunch?
Sit with the gardener. Ask how the plants are.
The gardener mentions something's been moving in the greenhouse leaves. What is it?
Someone new sits down at the table. Who are they?
Tell them about the gear piece. What do they make of it?
They say it might be from the sealed-off dome. Ask them to explain.
After lunch, head back to the greenhouse to investigate the moving thing.
Look closely at the leaves.
A small plastic pest scurries out. Catch it.
Give the pest to the gardener. Suggest cooling the greenhouse below 15°C to keep more pests out.
The gardener thanks me and offers a small cactus. Take it.
Head through the corridor — stiff-leg steps clicking on the plastic floor.
Back in the kitchen. The unclaimed package is still there. Open it.
Inside the package is a small tool. Add it to my toolkit.
Head to the airlock. I want to check outside the domes.
Suit up. How long does an oxygen suit last?
Step outside. Walk in silence across the regolith toward the solar array.
Examine the solar panels.
Use my screwdriver to tighten a loose panel.
Turn and look back at the domes from outside.
The hairline crack I saw this morning — is it visible from out here? Walk closer.
Move closer to the crack. Is anyone already working on patching it?
Try to linger outside longer to watch them finish the repair.
Head back inside before oxygen runs out. Remove the suit.
Tell the operations chief about what I saw at the cracked dome. Ask about the sealed dome.
Request permission to enter the sealed dome.
What does the chief say?
Suit up again. Enter the sealed dome.
Look around inside. What's in here?
Search for anything that matches the gear piece.
I find an old machine with a missing part. Try the gear piece.
The gear fits. What does the machine do?
Leave the sealed dome. Remove the suit.
Find the mechanic and tell them what I saw in the sealed dome.
The mechanic's stiff arms wave in excitement. What do they say?
Ask if we can take the rover back so the mechanic can see the machine.
My morning guide walks in and wants to come too. Remind them the rover only fits two.
Leave the guide behind. Take the rover with just the mechanic.
A draft through the airlock pops someone's hat off. Click it back on their head.
After showing the mechanic the machine, head back to the canteen for dinner.
At dinner the humming is faintly audible through these walls too.
Who's at dinner? List everyone I recognize.
Sit near the gardener. Ask how the plants are and whether there are more pests.
The operations chief stands up to make an announcement.
What's the announcement about?
The chief says Pip has been found inside the sealed dome, alive — they'd been working on the machine when it was sealed. How do people react?
Watch the mechanic's reaction — eyes wide, stiff arms frozen mid-bite.
Someone across the table pops off their own hair piece to scratch their head, then clicks it back on.
Someone asks to borrow a tool from my toolkit. Give them the wrench but nothing else.
What's in my toolkit now?
After dinner, head to the rec dome to unwind.
Try to write in my welcome packet about today with these claw hands.
Someone from dinner follows me in. Who?
They tell me something surprising about the operations chief. What?
Head back through the corridor toward my quarters.
Glance out the corridor window. Is the pulsing green light still out there?
Reach my quarters. Put the cactus on my shelf.
Full inventory check. What am I still carrying?
Lie down in bed. The humming is still faint through the wall. Think about everyone I met today.
